steps.title: Use the first two years
steps.content: The population starts at 2,000 animals, so use $(0,2000)$. A 3% increase gives $2000(1.03)=2060$ after one year, so use $(1,2060)$. Fit $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Read the growth factor
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=2000$ and $b=1.03$, so the function is $$p(x)=2000(1.03)^x.$$

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice C is the only equation with starting value 2,000 and yearly multiplier 1.03. Choice D would be a 3% decrease, choice A uses 3 as the whole multiplier, and choice B uses 1.97. Choice C is correct.
